descriptions: The Errol Flynns are recognized as the precursors to these successor gangs.
edges.evidence_quote: Some of the most notable successors include the "Be Like Boys", "Dexter Boys" (an offshoot of YBI), "Schoolcraft Boys or SCB's", "SNS", "Fenkell Boys", "FMK" ("Fenkell Mafia Killers"), "7 Mile Killers or 7 Mile Dogs", "Linwood Boys", "Brewster Boys", "Jeffries Boys", and "8 Mile sconys", ("JBFL") "Joy Boys" or "Exit 9" All of these crews, excepting the "Be Like Boys" gang, are named after Detroit city streets or housing projects and some of these gangs still exist under new leadership.

Rinaldi clan.description: The Rinaldi clan is a Camorra clan based in San Giovanni a Teduccio, Naples, founded in 1989 after splitting from the Mazzarella clan. It is involved in murder, extortion, drug trafficking, racketeering, and counterfeiting. Since 2019, it has formed a powerful alliance with the De Luca Bossa and Aprea-Cuccaro clans, dominating eastern Naples. The clan is allied with the Secondigliano Alliance and remains active.

descriptions: The Aprea-Cuccaro clan is allied with the Rinaldi clan.
edges.evidence_quote: According to the latest DIA reports, the Aprea-Cuccaro clan is allied with the Rinaldi clan from San Giovanni a Teduccio , and with the De Luca Bossa clan from Ponticelli , with the intention of undermining the Mazzarella clan.

descriptions: The Aprea-Cuccaro clan formed a criminal alliance with the De Luca Bossa clan and the Rinaldi clan.
edges.evidence_quote: the new criminal alliance formed with the De Luca Bossa clan and the Rinaldi clan

descriptions: De Luca Bossa allied with Rinaldi.
edges.evidence_quote: alleandosi con gli Aprea di Barra e ai Rinaldi di San Giovanni a Teduccio

edges.evidence_quote: the new criminal alliance formed with the De Luca Bossa clan and the Rinaldi clan

descriptions: The De Luca Bossa clan formed an alliance with the Rinaldi clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Minichini was known to have a good relationship with Ciro Rinaldi, boss of the Rinaldi clan, also based in the eastern part of Naples, making an alliance between the two clans.

Detroit Partnership.description: The Detroit Partnership is an Italian American Mafia crime family based in Detroit, Michigan, founded around 1908 by Pietro Mirabile. It operates primarily in the Greater Detroit area, with additional territory in Ohio, Ontario, and Las Vegas. The family has been involved in racketeering, gambling, loan-sharking, labor racketeering, narcotics trafficking, and other illegal activities. Despite RICO prosecutions in the 1990s, the organization remains active in the 21st century.

edges.evidence_quote: the leaders of other families, such as... Detroit

Södertälje mafia.description: Syriac-Aramean criminal organization based in Södertälje, Sweden, founded in the early 1980s. It is structured around family and kinship ties, with no single leader. The group is involved in drug trafficking, murder, welfare fraud, extortion, and other crimes. It has been confirmed by Swedish courts and continues to grow.

descriptions: X-Team and Södertälje mafia fought a war over control of Södertälje's criminal market.
edges.evidence_quote: A conflict between Bernard 'Berno' Khouri, leader of the Södertälje mafia (Södertäljenätverket), and Dany Moussa, the founder of the X-Team in Södertälje, resulted in a war between the groups

Nuova Mala del Brenta.description: The Nuova Mala del Brenta is a mafia-type criminal organization based in the Veneto region of Italy, emerging in the late 1990s as a successor to the original Mala del Brenta. It is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, money laundering, and other crimes. The group is considered by Italian authorities to have all characteristics of a mafia organization under Article 416 bis-cp. It operates in Northeast Italy and has ties to Cosa Nostra and other Italian mafia groups.

descriptions: Cosa Nostra is listed as an ally of Nuova Mala del Brenta in the infobox.

descriptions: Camorra members operate as associates of Nuova Mala del Brenta.
edges.evidence_quote: Its members are exclusively native to the Veneto region, but with a number of associates operating in the region from Cosa Nostra, Camorra and it is believed from the Ndrangheta as well.

descriptions: Nuova Mala del Brenta is the successor of the original Mala del Brenta.

Potashes.description: The Potashes were a 19th-century Irish-American street gang active in Greenwich Village and the New York waterfront during the early to mid-1890s. Led by Red Shay Meehan, the gang was based near the Babbit Soap Factory on Washington Street. They terrorized the neighborhood and dominated Lower West Side Manhattan until the end of the 1890s, when they were displaced by the Hudson Dusters.

Messina Brothers.description: The Messina Brothers were five Maltese brothers who led a criminal organization in London from the 1930s to the 1950s, primarily involved in prostitution. They were born to Giuseppe Messina, who established brothels in Malta and Egypt. The brothers expanded their operations to London's Soho district, controlling a large network of prostitutes and bribing police. Their criminal activities declined after the 1950s.

descriptions: Los Caparrapos have pacts with Los Pachenca to target Clan del Golfo.
edges.evidence_quote: ya tiene pactos con "Los Pachenca" para dar de baja a cualquier integrante del Clan del Golfo

descriptions: The Pirus allied with the Brims to oppose the Crips.
edges.evidence_quote: the 300 Playboy Piru Bloods formed an alliance with other smaller street gangs such as the Brims, Bishops and Denver Lanes, which also opposed the Crips.

Honeymoon Gang.description: The Honeymoon Gang was a mid-19th century New York street gang based in the East Side's 18th Ward. Known for brutal attacks on passersby, they operated with impunity until 1853 when police Captain George W. Walling formed the Strong Arm Squad to suppress them. The gang was disbanded within two weeks, with members fleeing the district.

Guardiões do Estado.description: Brazilian criminal faction originating in Fortaleza, Ceará, founded on January 1, 2016. It was the second largest faction in Ceará state, involved in drug trafficking, robberies, extortion, and rebellions. The group gained notoriety for orchestrating waves of attacks against public and private property in 2017 and 2019, allying with the PCC and Bonde dos 40 against the Comando Vermelho. It became defunct in 2025.
